items.title: Juiced Bikes is back with its moto-style Scrambler hardtail + full suspension e-bikes and $245 FREE bundles from $1,699
items.summary:
One of the staples of America’s e-bike market, Juiced Bikes, is officially back from the dead, thanks to Lectric’s 11th-hour save during 2024’s financial turmoil, and it’s kicking off its rebirth with the launch of two new e-bikes! You can now score the Scrambler Moto-Style Hardtail e-bike at $1,699 shipped, while the Scrambler Moto-Style Full Suspension e-bike sits a little higher at $1,899 shipped – and both models come with a $245 bundle of FREE gear. These new rides seem to be following the usual Lectric playbook of offering discounts on the bundled gear rather than the e-bikes themselves, with these bundles including a personalized nameplate, a headplate, a bar bag, a bar pad, a fender set, and a sticker pack.

Whether you’re planning a long weekend BBQ, upgrading the patio setup, or just looking for an excuse to finally grab that big-ticket outdoor item sitting in your cart, now’s the time to shop. items.title: Lectric launches new XPress2 cruiser + commuter e-bikes with FREE $336 gear bundle and loads of upgrades at $1,399
items.summary:
Following the surprise first-time discount on Aventon’s Current ADV eMTB, Lectric is now launching its newest second-generation XPress2 Long-Range Cruiser e-bike with a $336 FREE bundle of gear for $1,399 shipped, while the High-Step model is getting the moniker of a commuter e-bike. As you probably already know, Lectric very rarely cuts the actual prices on its e-bikes and instead discounts the bundled add-on gear, which would have this bundle package at $1,735 if that were not the case. It’s the average bundle size we normally see on the first-generation XPress e-bikes, containing a rear cargo rack, fender set, Elite headlight, and a suspension seatpost. We’re no longer seeing the Step-Through XPress1 on the website, so it’s safe to assume that once stock runs out on the high-step model, they will be fully replaced by this newer variation – and it’s only sitting $100 higher in price. Head below to learn more about it.

more…

items.title: Elon Musk said Sam Altman ‘stole’ a non-profit — but the trial showed he had similar aims
items.summary: The jury's speedy decision to reject Elon Musk's lawsuit against the other founders of OpenAI and Microsoft confirmed what we saw in the courtroom: Musk's case was a weak one, in part because he waited so long to file it.

items.title: Tested: This Hall effect aluminum unibody keyboard has bold looks backed by some serious gaming chops
items.summary:
In a world where most keyboards have started looking increasingly similar, all following more or less the same design language, it always catches my attention when something with a more aggressive gaming aesthetic – sharp angles, bold cuts, and a bit more personality – shows up every now and then. The IQUNIX EV63 Hall effect keyboard we’re checking out today is one of those, currently sitting on my desk. Not only does it look good but it also checks a lot of the right boxes when it comes to gaming. I’ve spent enough time using it to get a solid feel for what it brings to the table, so head below for a closer look and to learn more about my experience with it.

items.title: Google’s new Universal Cart wants to follow your entire shopping journey across the internet

Over at Amazon you can currently find the EGO Power+ FN1000 10-Inch 1,700 CFM Portable Oscillating Misting Fan down at $169 shipped. Normally $199 at full price, which is where it’s been keeping since mid-December, discounts before this drought period only took the price down as low as $172. You can also currently find the larger 18-inch Portable Misting Fan down at $259 from its full $309 pricing, while other tool deals from this brand can be found in the official Amazon storefront here.

This is regularly a $120 MagSafe charging stand, which is now seeing 45% discount. Today’s deal takes over $53 off this charger’s usual going rate, marking the lowest price we have tracked for it to date. There are a few different versions of this charger, and you’re looking at one of the latest models that ships with a 40W adapter and a 5-foot cable. You can also get this in white, though it’s currently going for $90 with a relatively lighter deal. Head below for more details.

items.title: Save up to 53% on Hiboy EVs for Memorial Day, like the budget-friendly S2 e-scooter at a $300 2026 low or more from $423
items.summary:
Hiboy has launched its Memorial Day Sale running through the upcoming holiday and into next week with up to 53% EV discounts and 25% accessory discounts. One of the most popular models amongst the bunch is the tried-and-true Hiboy S2 Electric Scooter that sits as the most affordable option during this sale at $299.99 shipped, matching its Amazon pricing. Don’t be fooled by the $600 MSRP it’s been given by the brand, as it’s only really been seen climbing as high as $353 since 2026 began, with the year also regularly seeing it discounted to this same rate. While we have seen it go lower in the past when tariffs weren’t as heavy, this is still a solid $53 markdown to the lowest price we have tracked for quite some time. Head below to learn about this budget-friendly commuter and browse the entire lineup of deals.
